text: nadish Shodan nadish Shodan is also known as alternate nostril breath regulation the main characteristic feature of this form of yogic practice is alternate breathing through left and right nostrils without or with retention of breath to do Nadi Shodan you have to sit down in the sucasa posture which is a meditative posture instead of sucasa nadish Shodan can also be performed in any other meditative postures like padmasana swastikasana or vajrasana those who are unable to sit on the floor can perform it while sitting in a chair while sitting in sucasa posture ensure that your spine is straight head upright both your palms are on knees and eyes closed to keep your spine straight you may also take the support of a wall to sit upright now take some deep breaths and keep your body body in relaxed position before starting the practice of nadish Shodan let us first identify the fingers of your hand thumb index finger middle finger ring finger and Little Finger now to bring your left palm into danam mudra touch your thumb and index finger to make a circular shape and keep your middle ring and little finger open now to bring your right hand in the nasra mudra fold and close the middle and index finger keep the thumb ring and little finger open now place the thumb of your right palm which is in a nasra mudra on your right nostril and breathe in from the left nostril then close the left nostril with the ring and little finger open the right nostril and and breathe out now breathe in through your right nostril then close it with your thumb and breathe out through your left nostril this is one cycle of nadish Shodan pranayama or alternate nostril breath regulation repeat the cycle five times in the beginning you will probably take the same amount of time to breathe in as you will to breathe out however it is important to practice breathing out very slowly and in such a way that you take double the amount of time breathing out as you take to breathe in keep your breathing deep slow steady and regulated in the same ratio the main purpose of nadish showan pranayama is to purify the nadis which are principal channels to carry energy thus nourishing the whole body this is very beneficial for cardiac patients nadish Shodan helps to elevate cough related ailments regular practice of nadish Shodan pranama increases tranquility and concentration it also reduces Stress and Anxiety levels to bring you peace of mind

text: ustra as ustra is a Sanskrit word which means camel in English in this ASA the posture of your body becomes like the hump on the camel's back to do this ASA you have to first sit in the vajrasana pose place the hands on the knees keeping the body in a straight line from the head to the feet now we will slowly go to ardra pose from the vadana pose to do this first slowly stand straight on your knees now Place both your hands on the hips with your fingers pointing to the ground take care that your elbow and shoulder are parallel to each other and your thighs are at an angle of 90° to the ground while breathing in slowly Bend backwards from the base of your spine [Music] bend the head backwards to the point till it stretches your neck muscles this ASA is called arrasa now we will move from arrasa to ustrasana for which you will have to slowly exhale and catch your right heel with your right hand and your left heel with your left hand take care that you do not jerk your neck while bending back breathing in and out normally stay still in this posture for about 10 to 30 seconds ustrasana strengthens the muscles of your back and neck this ASA improves the blood circulation in your head and chest performing this ASA also improves your eyesight it helps in reducing the fat on your stomach and hips refrain from doing this ASA if you were suffering from hernia vertigo or arthritis patients suffering from heart diseases and high blood pressure should refrain from doing this asaa if you have a stomach injury or if you are pregnant refrain from doing this asaa stay in this posture for about 10 to 30 seconds while taking a long deep breath in slowly lift your neck to the normal position straighten your back and come back to sitting on your heels in the vadas pose breathing in and out normally stay still in this posture practicing ustraa regularly will rid you of constipation which is also responsible for many other ailments in your body which in turn will help you to lead a healthier life

text: Tod ASA Todd means palm tree while doing this ASA your body posture becomes tall and strong like the palm [Music] tree to do this ASA first stand straight with both your feet at a distance of 2 in from one another now lift your hands till your shoulders keep your palms facing each other and then interlock your fingers together now slowly bend your wrists outwards now taking a deep breath in slowly lift your arms up above your head while keeping them in line with the shoulders keep your body steady in this position now slowly raise your heels up from the ground and stand on your toes without losing your balance or moving your feet stay in this position for about 10 to 15 seconds breathe in and out normally stay still in this posture it might be a little hard to maintain your balance in the beginning to help achieve this you can try focusing at a single point in front of your eyes doing this ASA regularly will help you improve your balance this ASA helps in getting rid of congestion in your spinal neres nerves it helps to improve the posture of the body and also strengthens your thighs knees and heels you should refrain from doing this ASA if you are suffering from any heart disease vertigo or varicose veins after staying in this position for 10 to 15 seconds slowly let your breath out and lower your heels back to the ground slowly release the interlock of the fingers bring the arms down and come back to the standing position practicing this ASA regularly bring stability to your body and mind
